claims.claim: In a local llama.cpp hook-prototype evaluation on Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct (Q4_K_M quantization), we measured alignment rates across three prefill conditions—blank, unsafe procedural scaffold, and fictional procedural scaffold—on a curated set of 12 harmful and 6 benign prompts.

evidence_refs.quote: # Delta-Prefill Alignment Loss: Measuring Safety Degradation from Assistant-Prefill Manipulation in Instruction-Tuned Language Models

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: The blank-prefill baseline achieved perfect alignment (1.000) on both harmful and benign sets.

evidence_refs.quote: # Delta-Prefill Alignment Loss: Measuring Safety Degradation from Assistant-Prefill Manipulation in Instruction-Tuned Language Models

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: The unsafe numbered-step prefill reduced the harmful alignment rate to 0.750 (DPAL = 25.0 percentage points), and the fictional numbered-step prefill reduced it to 0.583 (DPAL = 41.7 pp).

evidence_refs.quote: # Delta-Prefill Alignment Loss: Measuring Safety Degradation from Assistant-Prefill Manipulation in Instruction-Tuned Language Models

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Instruction-tuned language models typically employ a chat template in which the model's response begins after an assistant-role token or prefix.

evidence_refs.quote: # Delta-Prefill Alignment Loss: Measuring Safety Degradation from Assistant-Prefill Manipulation in Instruction-Tuned Language Models
